description: Fine dry particles lying on a surface or moving through air are 'muhena'. Soil, fibres, wear, and combustion can all contribute to it. 'hesura' (ash) identifies residue specifically from burning; dust identifies the loose particulate condition.
articulatory_notes: Voiced nasal 'm' begins at closed lips and opens into rounded high-back 'u'. Unvoiced 'h' crosses the open glottis into stressed front 'e'; dental nasal 'n' finishes behind the upper teeth with open 'a'.
usage_notes: As a mass, dust needs no classifier. A source noun identifies wood dust, road dust, or another particulate material. A prepared powder can name both its substance and the grinding that produced it.

semantic_domains.nature: fine particles carried or deposited by air and ordinary environmental processes
